Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx

Class FlixelCamera

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelCamera
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelUpdatable
public class FlixelCamera extends FlixelBasic
A powerful camera class that allows you to control the camera's position, zoom, and more.

In a full FlixelGDX game, cameras are usually managed by FlixelGame. You can also use FlixelCamera in a plain libGDX ApplicationListener. When Flixel.getGame() is null, window size and follow framerate fall back to Gdx.graphics (call update(int, int, boolean) from resize as usual).

Every camera wraps a libGDX Camera and Viewport internally. By default, an OrthographicCamera and FitViewport are used, but custom types can be provided via the constructor overloads.

FitViewport scales the game world to the window, so the world-to-screen factor is often not a whole number when the window is larger than the camera's internal size (e.g. fullscreen). libGDX BitmapFont defaults to integer-snapped glyph quads, which looks blocky under that scaling. Set BitmapFont.setUseIntegerPositions(boolean) to false on fonts you draw through this pipeline (FlixelGDX does this for FlixelText and registry fonts automatically).

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    FlixelCamera.FollowStyle
    Determines how a FlixelCamera follows a FlixelObject.
    static enum 
    FlixelCamera.RegionMode
    Defines how a FlixelCamera's screen region coordinates are interpreted when placing its viewport on the window.

    The color tint of the camera display.

    The dead zone rectangle, measured from the camera's upper left corner in game pixels.
    static float
    defaultZoom
    Any FlixelCamera with a zoom of anything <= 0 (the default constructor value) will receive this zoom level instead.

    followLead
    Used to force the camera to look ahead of the target.
    float
    followLerp
    The ratio of the distance to the follow target the camera moves per 1/60 sec.

    maxScrollX

    Float

    Lower bound of the camera's scroll on the X axis.
    Float

    Lower bound of the camera's scroll on the Y axis.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    FlixelCamera()
    Creates a camera sized to the current window using the default OrthographicCamera and FitViewport.
    FlixelCamera(float x, float y, int width, int height, float zoom)
    Creates a camera at the given display position, size, and zoom level using default types.
    FlixelCamera(float x, float y, int width, int height, float zoom, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Camera camera, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.viewport.Viewport viewport)
    Full constructor allowing fully custom libGDX Camera and Viewport types.
    FlixelCamera(int width, int height)
    Creates a camera with the given dimensions using the default camera and viewport types.
    FlixelCamera(int width, int height, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Camera camera)
    Creates a camera with a custom libGDX Camera, wrapped in a default FitViewport.
    FlixelCamera(int width, int height, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.viewport.Viewport viewport)
    Creates a camera with a custom libGDX Viewport.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    apply()
    Applies the viewport's OpenGL viewport rectangle.
    void
    applyLibCameraTransform()
    Pushes scroll, zoom, angle, and shake into the underlying libGDX Camera.
    com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2
    bindScrollPos(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 scrollPos)
    Clamps the given scroll position to this camera's min/max bounds, modifying it in-place.
    void
    changeZoom(float zoom)
    Changes the zoom level by the given amount.
    void
    clearScreenRegion()
    Clears the custom pixel region set by setScreenRegion(float, float, int, int).
    boolean
    containsPoint(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 point)
    Checks whether this camera's display area contains the given point (screen coordinates).
    boolean
    containsPoint(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 point, float width, float height)
    Checks whether this camera's display area overlaps a rectangle at the given point.
    boolean
    containsRect(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Rectangle rect)
    Checks whether this camera's display area overlaps the given rectangle (screen coordinates).
    FlixelCamera
    copyFrom(FlixelCamera other)
    Copies the bounds, follow target, deadzone info, and scroll from another camera.
    void
    destroy()
    Cleans up this camera's state, stopping all effects and clearing the follow target.
    void
    drawFX(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture whitePixel)
    Draws active screen effects (flash and fade overlays) using the given batch.

    Fades to black over 1 second.
    void
    fade(@NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
    Fades to the given color over 1 second.
    void
    fade(@NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration)
    Fades to the given color over the specified duration.
    void
    fade(@NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration, boolean fadeIn)
    Fades to/from the given color.
    void
    fade(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration, boolean fadeIn, Runnable onComplete, boolean force)
    The screen is gradually filled with (or cleared of) this color.
    void
    fill(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color fillColor, boolean blendAlpha, float fxAlpha, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture whitePixel)
    Fills the camera display with the specified color using the given batch and a 1x1 white Texture.

    Flashes white for 1 second.

    Flashes the given color for the specified duration.

    The screen is filled with this color and gradually returns to normal.

    Stops all screen effects (flash, fade, shake) on this camera.

  • Field Details

    • defaultZoom

      public static float defaultZoom
      Any FlixelCamera with a zoom of anything <= 0 (the default constructor value) will receive this zoom level instead.

    • alpha

      public float alpha
      The alpha value of this camera display (0.0 to 1.0).

    • angle

      public float angle
      The angle of the camera display in degrees.

    • bgColor

      public com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color bgColor
      The natural background color of the camera. Defaults to black.

    • color

      public com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color
      The color tint of the camera display.

    • pixelPerfectRender

      public boolean pixelPerfectRender
      Whether positions of rendered objects are rounded to whole pixels.

    • pixelPerfectShake

      public boolean pixelPerfectShake
      If true, screen shake offsets will be rounded to whole pixels. If null, pixelPerfectRender is used instead.

    • useBgAlphaBlending

      public boolean useBgAlphaBlending
      Whether to use alpha blending for the camera's background fill.

    • centerCameraOnResize

      public boolean centerCameraOnResize
      Whether the libGDX viewport should (re-)center the camera when the game window is resized.

      Split-screen setups often want this to stay enabled (default), matching existing behavior. Disable it if you want strict scroll preservation through resizes.

    • x

      public float x
      The X position of this camera's display in native screen pixels. zoom does NOT affect this value.

    • y

      public float y
      The Y position of this camera's display in native screen pixels. zoom does NOT affect this value.

    • width

      public int width
      How wide the camera display is, in game pixels.

    • height

      public int height
      How tall the camera display is, in game pixels.

    • scroll

      public final com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 scroll
      The basic parallax scrolling values, essentially the camera's top-left corner position in world coordinates. Use focusOn(Vector2) to look at a specific world point.

    • minScrollX

      public Float minScrollX
      Lower bound of the camera's scroll on the X axis. null = unbounded.

    • maxScrollX

      public Float maxScrollX
      Upper bound of the camera's scroll on the X axis. null = unbounded.

    • minScrollY

      public Float minScrollY
      Lower bound of the camera's scroll on the Y axis. null = unbounded.

    • maxScrollY

      public Float maxScrollY
      Upper bound of the camera's scroll on the Y axis. null = unbounded.

    • deadzone

      public com.badlogic.gdx.math.Rectangle deadzone
      The dead zone rectangle, measured from the camera's upper left corner in game pixels. The camera will always keep the focus object inside this zone unless bumping against scroll bounds. For rapid prototyping, use the preset styles with follow(FlixelObject, FollowStyle, float).

    • followLead

      public final com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 followLead
      Used to force the camera to look ahead of the target.

    • followLerp

      public float followLerp
      The ratio of the distance to the follow target the camera moves per 1/60 sec. 1.0 = snap to target. 0.0 = don't move. Lower values produce smoother motion.

    • style

      public FlixelCamera.FollowStyle style
      The current follow style.

    • target

      public FlixelObject target
      The FlixelObject the camera follows. Set via follow(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject).

    • targetOffset

      public final com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 targetOffset
      Offset applied to the follow target's position.

    • initialZoom

      public final float initialZoom
      Camera's initial zoom value, captured at construction time.

    • useSubScreenViewport

      public boolean useSubScreenViewport
      When true, update(int, int, boolean) fits this camera into the screen rectangle (x, y, width, height) (see x) instead of the full window. When false, placement is inferred when Flixel.getGame() has multiple cameras (horizontal/vertical strips, PiP, etc.).

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelCamera

      public FlixelCamera()
      Creates a camera sized to the current window using the default OrthographicCamera and FitViewport.

    • FlixelCamera

      public FlixelCamera(int width, int height)
      Creates a camera with the given dimensions using the default camera and viewport types.
      Parameters:
      width - The width of the camera display in game pixels.
      height - The height of the camera display in game pixels.

    • FlixelCamera

      public FlixelCamera(int width, int height, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Camera camera)
      Creates a camera with a custom libGDX Camera, wrapped in a default FitViewport.
      Parameters:
      width - The width of the camera display in game pixels.
      height - The height of the camera display in game pixels.
      camera - A custom libGDX Camera (e.g. PerspectiveCamera).

    • FlixelCamera

      public FlixelCamera(int width, int height, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.viewport.Viewport viewport)
      Creates a camera with a custom libGDX Viewport. The camera is extracted from the viewport.
      Parameters:
      width - The width of the camera display in game pixels.
      height - The height of the camera display in game pixels.
      viewport - A custom libGDX Viewport (e.g. ScreenViewport).

    • FlixelCamera

      public FlixelCamera(float x, float y, int width, int height, float zoom)
      Creates a camera at the given display position, size, and zoom level using default types.
      Parameters:
      x - X location of the camera's display in native screen pixels.
      y - Y location of the camera's display in native screen pixels.
      width - The width of the camera display in game pixels. 0 = window width.
      height - The height of the camera display in game pixels. 0 = window height.
      zoom - The initial zoom level. 0 = defaultZoom.

    • FlixelCamera

      public FlixelCamera(float x, float y, int width, int height, float zoom, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Camera camera, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.viewport.Viewport viewport)
      Full constructor allowing fully custom libGDX Camera and Viewport types.

      If viewport is provided, its camera is used (the camera parameter is ignored). If only camera is provided, it is wrapped in a new FitViewport. If neither is provided, an OrthographicCamera and FitViewport are created.

      Parameters:
      x - X location of the camera's display in native screen pixels.
      y - Y location of the camera's display in native screen pixels.
      width - The width of the camera display in game pixels. 0 = window width.
      height - The height of the camera display in game pixels. 0 = window height.
      zoom - The initial zoom level. 0 = defaultZoom. 2 = 2x magnification.
      camera - Custom libGDX Camera, or null for a default OrthographicCamera.
      viewport - Custom libGDX Viewport, or null for a default FitViewport.

  • Method Details

    • getCamera

      public com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Camera getCamera()
      Returns the underlying libGDX Camera used for projection.

    • getViewport

      public com.badlogic.gdx.utils.viewport.Viewport getViewport()
      Returns the underlying libGDX Viewport used for screen scaling.

    • apply

      public void apply()
      Applies the viewport's OpenGL viewport rectangle. Call before rendering through this camera.

    • update

      public void update(int screenWidth, int screenHeight, boolean centerCamera)
      Updates the viewport in response to a window resize event.
      Parameters:
      screenWidth - The new screen width in pixels.
      screenHeight - The new screen height in pixels.
      centerCamera - Whether to re-center the camera in the viewport.

    • getWorldWidth

      public float getWorldWidth()

    • getWorldHeight

      public float getWorldHeight()

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Updates the camera scroll, follow logic, and active effects. Called once per frame from the game loop.
      Specified by:
      update in interface FlixelUpdatable
      Overrides:
      update in class FlixelBasic
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds elapsed since the last frame.

    • applyLibCameraTransform

      public void applyLibCameraTransform()
      Pushes scroll, zoom, angle, and shake into the underlying libGDX Camera. Call this after scroll is mutated outside update(float) (e.g. debug pause pan) and before Viewport.unproject(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2) or rendering. Safe to call every frame; update(float) ends with this.

      Drawables use scroll-local batch coordinates (worldPos - scroll, see FlixelSprite.draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch)). The orthographic camera must look at the center of that space (viewW/2, viewH/2), not scroll + view/2, or the frustum disagrees with batch vertices whenever scroll != 0 (broken follow, split-screen drift).

    • follow

      public void follow(FlixelObject target)
      Tells this camera to follow the given sprite using FlixelCamera.FollowStyle.LOCKON and lerp of 1.0f.
      Parameters:
      target - The sprite to follow. Pass null to stop following.

    • follow

      public void follow(FlixelObject target, FlixelCamera.FollowStyle style)
      Tells this camera to follow the given sprite with the specified style and a lerp of 1.0f.
      Parameters:
      target - The sprite to follow. Pass null to stop following.
      style - One of the preset FlixelCamera.FollowStyle dead zone presets.

    • follow

      public void follow(FlixelObject target, FlixelCamera.FollowStyle style, float lerp)
      Tells this camera to follow the given sprite.
      Parameters:
      target - The sprite to follow. Pass null to stop following.
      style - One of the preset FlixelCamera.FollowStyle dead zone presets.
      lerp - How much lag the camera should have. 1.0f = snap, lower = smoother.

    • focusOn

      public void focusOn(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 point)
      Instantly moves the camera so the given world point is centered.
      Parameters:
      point - The world-space point to focus on.

    • snapToTarget

      public void snapToTarget()
      Snaps the camera to the current target position with no easing, then clamps scroll to bounds. Useful after teleporting the target.

    • setScrollBounds

      public void setScrollBounds(Float minX, Float maxX, Float minY, Float maxY)
      Specifies the bounds of where the camera scroll is allowed. Pass null for any side to leave it unbounded.
      Parameters:
      minX - Lower X bound (or null).
      maxX - Upper X bound (or null).
      minY - Lower Y bound (or null).
      maxY - Upper Y bound (or null).

    • setScrollBoundsRect

      public void setScrollBoundsRect(float x, float y, float w, float h)
      Specifies scroll bounds as a bounding rectangle (typically the level size).
      Parameters:
      x - Smallest X value (usually 0).
      y - Smallest Y value (usually 0).
      w - Largest X extent (usually level width).
      h - Largest Y extent (usually level height).

    • setScrollBoundsRect

      public void setScrollBoundsRect(float x, float y, float w, float h, boolean updateWorld)
      Specifies scroll bounds as a bounding rectangle.
      Parameters:
      x - Smallest X value (usually 0).
      y - Smallest Y value (usually 0).
      w - Largest X extent (usually level width).
      h - Largest Y extent (usually level height).
      updateWorld - Reserved for future use (quad-tree bounds).

    • updateScroll

      public void updateScroll()
      Clamps the current scroll to the configured scroll bounds. Called automatically each frame by update(float).

    • bindScrollPos

      public com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 bindScrollPos(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 scrollPos)
      Clamps the given scroll position to this camera's min/max bounds, modifying it in-place.
      Parameters:
      scrollPos - The scroll position to restrict.
      Returns:
      The same Vector2 passed in, clamped within bounds.

    • flash

      public void flash()
      Flashes white for 1 second.

    • flash

      public void flash(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
      Flashes the given color for 1 second.
      Parameters:
      color - The color to flash.

    • flash

      public void flash(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration)
      Flashes the given color for the specified duration.
      Parameters:
      color - The color to flash.
      duration - How long the flash takes to fade, in seconds.

    • flash

      public void flash(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration, Runnable onComplete, boolean force)
      The screen is filled with this color and gradually returns to normal.
      Parameters:
      color - The color to flash.
      duration - How long the flash takes to fade, in seconds.
      onComplete - Callback invoked when the flash finishes, or null.
      force - If true, resets any currently running flash.

    • fade

      public void fade()
      Fades to black over 1 second.

    • fade

      public void fade(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
      Fades to the given color over 1 second.
      Parameters:
      color - The color to fade to.

    • fade

      public void fade(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration)
      Fades to the given color over the specified duration.
      Parameters:
      color - The color to fade to.
      duration - How long the fade takes to fade, in seconds.

    • fade

      public void fade(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration, boolean fadeIn)
      Fades to/from the given color.
      Parameters:
      color - The color to fade to/from.
      duration - How long the fade takes to fade, in seconds.
      fadeIn - true = fade FROM the color to clear. false = fade TO the color.

    • fade

      public void fade(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color, float duration, boolean fadeIn, Runnable onComplete, boolean force)
      The screen is gradually filled with (or cleared of) this color.
      Parameters:
      color - The color to fade to/from.
      duration - How long the fade takes, in seconds.
      fadeIn - true = fade FROM the color to clear. false = fade TO the color.
      onComplete - Callback invoked when the fade finishes, or null.
      force - If true, resets any currently-running fade.

    • shake

      public void shake()
      Shakes with default intensity (0.05) for 0.5 seconds on both axes.

    • shake

      public void shake(float intensity)
      Shakes with the given intensity for 0.5 seconds on both axes.
      Parameters:
      intensity - The intensity of the shake. This is typically VERY small numbers like 0.05f or 0.01f.

    • shake

      public void shake(float intensity, float duration)
      Shakes with the given intensity and duration on both axes.
      Parameters:
      intensity - The intensity of the shake. This is typically VERY small numbers like 0.05f or 0.01f.
      duration - How long the shake lasts, in seconds.

    • shake

      public void shake(float intensity, float duration, Runnable onComplete, boolean force, FlixelAxes axes)
      A simple screen-shake effect.
      Parameters:
      intensity - Fraction of camera size representing the max shake distance. This is typically VERY small numbers like 0.05f or 0.01f.
      duration - How long the shake lasts, in seconds.
      onComplete - Callback invoked when the shake finishes, or null.
      force - If true, resets any currently-running shake (default unlike flash/fade).
      axes - Which axes to shake on.

    • stopFX

      public void stopFX()
      Stops all screen effects (flash, fade, shake) on this camera.

    • stopFlash

      public void stopFlash()
      Stops the flash effect on this camera.

    • stopFade

      public void stopFade()
      Stops the fade effect on this camera.

    • stopShake

      public void stopShake()
      Stops the shake effect on this camera.

    • fill

      public void fill(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color fillColor, boolean blendAlpha, float fxAlpha, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture whitePixel)
      Fills the camera display with the specified color using the given batch and a 1x1 white Texture.
      Parameters:
      fillColor - The color to fill with (an alpha channel is respected).
      blendAlpha - Whether to blend the alpha or overwrite previous contents.
      fxAlpha - Additional alpha multiplier (0.0 to 1.0).
      batch - An active Batch to draw with (must be between begin() and end()).
      whitePixel - A 1x1 white Texture used for color drawing.

    • drawFX

      public void drawFX(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture whitePixel)
      Draws active screen effects (flash and fade overlays) using the given batch. Call this after drawing all game objects but before batch.end().
      Parameters:
      batch - An active Batch (must be between begin/end).
      whitePixel - A 1x1 white Texture used for color drawing.

    • containsPoint

      public boolean containsPoint(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 point)
      Checks whether this camera's display area contains the given point (screen coordinates).
      Parameters:
      point - The point to test.
      Returns:
      true if the point is inside the camera display.

    • containsPoint

      public boolean containsPoint(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 point, float width, float height)
      Checks whether this camera's display area overlaps a rectangle at the given point.
      Parameters:
      point - Top-left corner of the rectangle in screen coordinates.
      width - Width of the rectangle.
      height - Height of the rectangle.
      Returns:
      true if any part of the rectangle overlaps the camera display.

    • containsRect

      public boolean containsRect(com.badlogic.gdx.math.Rectangle rect)
      Checks whether this camera's display area overlaps the given rectangle (screen coordinates).
      Parameters:
      rect - The rectangle to test.
      Returns:
      true if the rectangle overlaps the camera display.

    • getViewWidth

      public float getViewWidth()

    • getViewHeight

      public float getViewHeight()

    • getViewX

      public float getViewX()

    • getViewY

      public float getViewY()

    • getViewLeft

      public float getViewLeft()

    • getViewTop

      public float getViewTop()

    • getViewRight

      public float getViewRight()

    • getViewBottom

      public float getViewBottom()

    • getViewMarginX

      public float getViewMarginX()

    • getViewMarginY

      public float getViewMarginY()

    • getViewMarginLeft

      public float getViewMarginLeft()

    • getViewMarginRight

      public float getViewMarginRight()

    • getViewMarginTop

      public float getViewMarginTop()

    • getViewMarginBottom

      public float getViewMarginBottom()

    • getViewMarginRect

      public com.badlogic.gdx.math.Rectangle getViewMarginRect()

    • getZoom

      public float getZoom()

    • setZoom

      public void setZoom(float zoom)
      Sets the zoom level. 1 = 1:1, 2 = 2x magnification (world appears larger). Cameras always zoom toward their center.
      Parameters:
      zoom - The new zoom level.

    • restoreScrollAndZoom

      public void restoreScrollAndZoom(float scrollX, float scrollY, float zoomLevel)
      Restores scroll and zoom together without running setZoom(float)'s recentering logic. Used when leaving debug pause so inspect-tool mutations can be reverted exactly as they were before.
      Parameters:
      scrollX - World scroll X to restore.
      scrollY - World scroll Y to restore.
      zoomLevel - Zoom to restore; non-positive values are replaced with defaultZoom.

    • changeZoom

      public void changeZoom(float zoom)
      Changes the zoom level by the given amount.
      Parameters:
      zoom - The amount to change the zoom level by.

    • getScaleX

      public float getScaleX()

    • getScaleY

      public float getScaleY()

    • getTotalScaleX

      public float getTotalScaleX()

    • getTotalScaleY

      public float getTotalScaleY()

    • setPosition

      public void setPosition(float x, float y)

    • setRegionMode

      public void setRegionMode(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelCamera.RegionMode regionMode)
      Sets the screen-region mode for this camera.

      This changes how x/y and region sizes are interpreted when placing the viewport rectangle on screen. It does not change world-space object movement, physics, or camera follow math.

      Parameters:
      regionMode - The new region mode. null is ignored.

    • getRegionMode

      public FlixelCamera.RegionMode getRegionMode()
      Returns the current screen-region mode.

    • setScreenRegion

      public void setScreenRegion(float x, float y, int width, int height)
      Sets the camera's screen rectangle in pixels.

      Interpretation depends on regionMode: top-left anchored, bottom-left anchored, or center anchored.

      Parameters:
      x - Region X coordinate in pixels.
      y - Region Y coordinate in pixels.
      width - Region width in pixels.
      height - Region height in pixels.

    • setScreenRegionNormalized

      public void setScreenRegionNormalized(float x, float y, float width, float height)
      Sets the camera's screen rectangle in normalized coordinates (0..1), relative to window size.

      This is used only when regionMode is FlixelCamera.RegionMode.NORMALIZED_RECT. Normalized coordinates use a top-left origin (Y increases downward) for consistency with HaxeFlixel-style layout.

      Parameters:
      x - Normalized X position (0..1).
      y - Normalized Y position (0..1).
      width - Normalized width (0..1).
      height - Normalized height (0..1).

    • clearScreenRegion

      public void clearScreenRegion()
      Clears the custom pixel region set by setScreenRegion(float, float, int, int).

      After clearing, pixel-based modes fall back to legacy fields (x, y, width, height).

    • setScale

      public void setScale(float scaleX, float scaleY)
      Sets the zoom-based scale of this camera. Because cameras use a single zoom value, this sets zoom to the average of scaleX and scaleY.
      Parameters:
      scaleX - The desired horizontal scale.
      scaleY - The desired vertical scale.

    • setSize

      public void setSize(int width, int height)
      Sets both width and height of the camera display.
      Parameters:
      width - The new width in game pixels.
      height - The new height in game pixels.

    • copyFrom

      public FlixelCamera copyFrom(FlixelCamera other)
      Copies the bounds, follow target, deadzone info, and scroll from another camera.
      Parameters:
      other - The camera to copy from.
      Returns:
      This camera for chaining.

    • onResize

      public void onResize()
      Called by the game's front-end on window resize. Triggers repositioning of internal display objects.

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Cleans up this camera's state, stopping all effects and clearing the follow target.
      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
      Overrides:
      destroy in class FlixelBasic
      See Also:
      • FlixelBasic.dispose()
      • FlixelBasic.reset()

    • isFlashActive

      public boolean isFlashActive()

    • isFadeActive

      public boolean isFadeActive()

    • isShakeActive

      public boolean isShakeActive()

    • getFlashColor

      public com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color getFlashColor()

    • getFlashAlpha

      public float getFlashAlpha()

    • getFadeColor

      public com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color getFadeColor()

    • getFadeAlpha

      public float getFadeAlpha()
